intro: Hieracium pseudobracteolatum, also known as false hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pseudobracteolatum include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium pseudobracteolatum is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to support joint health and reduce inflammation, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
